``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} # Parole Eligibility ## How Parole Eligibility is Determined The parole board by policy sets parole eligibility for many offenses: A maximum sentence of 40 years or more is eligible in 8 years; maximum terms of two to 39 years are eligible at the 20-percent; and sentences of one year and up to but not including two years are eligible at 4 months. By statute there is a 15 percent group for nonviolent Class D offenses and an 85 percent group for violent offenses.
## In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year In 2020, there were 5,475 people in prison past their parole consideration year. This group made up 48% of people in prison for new crimes and with sentence lengths between 1 to 25 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
### Demographics The demographics of people in prison past their parole consideration year are mostly notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and Black, non-Hispanic people. By gender, there were more males than females. Age-wise, most people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
### Criminal Offenses ::: {.cell} ::: In 2020, 59% of people in prison past their parole consideration year were in prison for non-violent offenses. The breakdown of criminal offenses reveals a more varied landscape, with most people incarcerated for drugs (22%) and property (22%) offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
### Original Sentence Lengths In 2020, most people in prison past their parole consideration year had original sentence lengths between 5 to 9.9 years representing 43% of those eligible for parole. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Missing Data This section will contain information related to missingness in the NCRP data. Keep?


# Prison Population ## Prison Population Trends From 2010 to 2022, the prison population decreased 10%, changing from 20,544 in 2010 to 18,560 in 2022. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners Series
## Demographics The demographics of people in prison are mostly Data on race and ethnicity is incomplete or missing. By gender, there were more males than females. Age-wise, most people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners 2022 - Race and Ethnicity
## Criminal Offenses In 2020, 56% of people in prison were incarcerated for non-violent offenses. The breakdown of criminal offenses reveals a more varied landscape, with most people incarcerated for drugs (24%) and property (20%) offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Sentence Lengths In 2020, most people in prison had original sentence lengths between 10 to 24.9 years representing 39%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Prison Releases ## Prison Release Trends From 2010 to 2020, prison releases increased 2%, dropping from 16,520 in 2010 to 16,868 in 2020. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
In 2020, 60% of people eligible for parole were released during their eligibility year. This represents a 2% increase compared to 2010. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Conditional Releases Conditional release involves an individual’s release under specific conditions and supervision, whereas unconditional release means the individual is released without further obligations or restrictions. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Demographics of People Released The demographics of people released from prison are mostly notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and black, non-hispanic people. By gender, there were more males than females. Age-wise, most people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. These findings provide insights into the populations transitioning back into the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Time Served by Offense Between 2010 and 2020, there were shifts in the average time served by individuals for different offense types in Kentucky. The largest change was for robbery offenses, which increased by 49%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Disparities ## Incarceration Rates In Kentucky, Black, non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 3.2 times higher than White, non-Hispanic people, when accounting for population sizes in the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::

## Timing of Release After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity The timing of release for individuals after their parole eligibility year varied by offense type and demographic group, with many remaining in prison for several years beyond their eligibility date. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender In progress. Same type of graph as race and ethnicity tab. :::
## Time Served ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity ::: {.cell} ::: Black people faced the longest average time served in prison in 2020, with an average of 2.1 years. White people experienced shorter prison stays, averaging 1.3 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: By offense type, disparities were observed in time served by race and ethnicity. For rape or sexual assault offenses, Black people had 2.7 more years on average compared to people of Other* races, who had the shortest time served for these offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender In progress. Same type of graph as race and ethnicity tab. :::
